desc_short: Not a faction. An ecology with teeth.
desc_long: The canyon was here first. Monsters are not an army—they are the reason Coffin Canyon exists. Native inhabitants who react to intruders with instinct, territory, and survival.
history: After the Catastrophe, the canyon's hidden layers woke up. Tainted ground drew hungry things. Haunted ground gathered the bloodless. Strangewild growth fed predators. Every extraction, every fire, every desperate camp—another invitation. The Monsters faction represents what happens when the canyon gets a vote.

what_they_fight_for: Protection of nests, warrens, and lairs
what_they_fight_against: Anyone who thinks the canyon belongs to them

reputation.allies_see_them_as: A volatile third party—useful chaos if you can survive it.
reputation.enemies_see_them_as: A constant threat and a shifting nightmare: too many kinds, too many rules.

faction_mechanics.signature_ability_description: Monsters are built to pressure scenarios: fast nuisances, ambushers, terror auras, and heavy bruisers. Some are Befriend-eligible and become scenario pieces (escorts, calms, redirects). Others are hard-locked as hostile threats that demand response.
faction_mechanics.playstyle: A toolbox of threats: cheap objective pests, stealth assassins, terror pieces, durable anchors, and occasional Titans. Monsters excel at disrupting plans, punishing overextension, and forcing Morale tests and positional mistakes.
strengths: Wide spread of roles and stat profiles
High ceiling with ambush and terrain play
Titans and large threats can swing games
weaknesses: Many units are CannotBeBefriended (no diplomacy angle)
Can struggle vs disciplined ranged gunlines
Some units are fragile and rely on timing and cover
Faction identity is threat-based, not objective-based by default

preferred_states.reason: Predators thrive, nests multiply, and the canyon grows teeth.
Bloodless and spirits become common, and the living make worse decisions.
Toxic weather and tainted ground favor the things that were built for it.

neutral_states.reason: Chaos invites monsters, but chaos also brings more guns.
Containment reduces roaming threats, but lairs still exist.

opposed_states.reason: Order, suppression, and industrial discipline reduce monster roaming and leverage.
Strip-mined wastelands remove the ecosystem—some monsters starve out, others escalate into pure violence.
Machine dominance and dead zones reduce living ecology (Bloodless may remain, but beasts suffer).

ideal_scenarios: Territory control with lairs and nests

faction_features.name: Native Deployment
faction_features.effect: Monsters deploy after all terrain is placed, but before other factions. They may deploy anywhere on the table, including inside ruins, near Thyr crystals, or in ambush positions.

faction_features.name: Monster Exits
faction_features.effect: Scenarios may designate Monster Exits (mines, caves, ruins, sewers, hidden paths). A Monster may leave the table via an Exit instead of activating normally. Exited Monsters are removed from play and may score Survival or Escape VP (scenario dependent).

faction_features.name: Temperament System
faction_features.effect: Every Monster has a Temperament (Friendly, Vengeful, Cunning, Territorial, Skittish, Stalwart) that affects how it reacts to danger and morale. See individual unit cards for specific behaviors.

faction_features.name: Befriending
faction_features.effect: Certain Monsters (marked CanBeBefriended) may be befriended, escorted, or temporarily allied with Monster Rangers. Others (marked CannotBeBefriended) react violently to such attempts.

victory_objectives.description: Monsters score VP for remaining alive at game end. Specific VP values are scenario-dependent.
Monsters score VP for remaining near nests, ruins, lairs, or designated territories. Territorial Monsters often defend specific areas.
Monsters score VP for holding or consuming Thyr crystals. Some Monsters are drawn to Thyr and gain bonuses when near it.
Monsters score VP for successfully exiting the table via Monster Exits. Represents survival and retreat rather than conquest.
Only certain predatory Monsters score VP for kills. Most Monsters do not score for combat.
Some Monsters lose points if killed, grant points to killers, or cause global VP shifts when destroyed (especially Titans).

composition_requirements.note: Monsters are scenario-driven and do not follow standard army composition rules. Scenarios determine which Monsters appear, how many, and their victory conditions.
composition_requirements.deployment_order: Monsters deploy after terrain placement but before other factions
composition_requirements.titan_rule: Titans are scenario events, not standard units. When a Titan enters play, all factions must reassess priorities.

weapons_note: Monster costs assume natural weapons and profile abilities. Weapon properties are listed when relevant. Monster abilities reference the defined ability dictionaries wherever possible.
universal_upgrades_note: Monsters typically do not use equipment upgrades. They are wild creatures, not soldiers. Individual Monsters may have scenario-specific upgrades, mutations, or escalations.

units.name: Canyon Goblin
units.lore: Canyon goblins scavenge old rails, broken wagons, and ruined camps. They know every crack in the stone, every shade line at noon, and every hiding place worth having.
units.tactics: Objective nuisance. Use them to screen, contest, and flee. They are not fighters.

supplemental_abilities.name: Red Goblin
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Circus goblins wear the red hat like a brand and iron shoes like a sentence. Fast, cruel, and difficult to pin down.

units.name: Jackalope
units.lore: The jackalope is the most famous lie ever told in the canyon—and somehow it keeps proving itself real. Horned rabbits ricochet across the desert floor faster than bullets, leaving only dust and disbelief behind.
units.tactics: Objective thief and battlefield chaos agent. Use speed and ricochet movement to contest objectives and escape danger.

optional_upgrades.name: Thyr-Touched Antlers
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Jackalope gains Fear. Its antlers glow faintly with Thyr energy.

optional_upgrades.name: Dust Mirage
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per round when this unit is targeted by a ranged attack, it may move 3" before the attack is resolved.

supplemental_abilities.name: Alpha Jackalope
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A massive horned jackalope rumored to lead desert herds. When it charges, it hits like a flying spear.

units.name: Canyon Harpy
units.lore: Canyon harpies roost in the high red cliffs, building nests of bones, broken bottles, and stolen jewelry. Their feathers stink of rot and carrion, and their claws carry the sickness of everything they have eaten.
units.tactics: Fast harassment predator. Fly over screens, strike weak targets, and retreat before heavier units can respond.

supplemental_abilities.name: Rotwing Harpy
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A diseased canyon harpy whose wings are mottled with rot and parasites. Its claws drip infection and its breath carries the stench of shallow graves.

units.name: Ridge Satyr
units.lore: Ridge satyrs haunt the dry groves and hidden springs of Coffin Canyon. With cracked pan pipes and crooked smiles, they play tunes that slip into the bones and make good sense wander off somewhere else. They are not cruel creatures—but they are terrible influences.
units.tactics: Control rogue. Hex dangerous enemies and disrupt formations with pipe-song. Avoid direct combat; satyrs win by mischief rather than strength.

optional_upgrades.name: Brass Bow
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Add a Brass Bow (Range 18"). A strange mechanical bow of brass limbs and tension springs that hums faintly when drawn.

supplemental_abilities.name: Moonridge Satyr
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A pale satyr that wanders the canyon by moonlight. Its pipe-song chills the heart and unsettles even hardened gunslingers.

units.name: Vendomat

composition.note: Scenario-placed only. Not drafted into rosters.

units.lore: The Vendomat appeared one morning without explanation — ten feet of clanking iron and carved pumpkin, wandering the canyon floor with a stock of supplies and a coin slot in its chest. Nobody knows who built it. Nobody knows how it restocks. Nobody attacks it twice. The blast radius is ten inches and the hatchet moves faster than it should.
units.tactics: The Vendomat wanders freely across the board at Move 6". Any faction can spend an action and 2 Gildren to buy supplies — first come, first served. Stock is limited. It will fight back if attacked, and its death explosion punishes the whole board. Smart players leave it alone and race to reach it.

units.name: Monte Haul

composition.note: Scenario-placed only. Not drafted into rosters.

units.lore: Monte Haul is a brass mechan in the shape of a broad-shouldered merchant, spring-mounted to a heavy base, with a coin slot in his menu panel and a permanent grin cast into his faceplate. He sells things that probably shouldn't exist. He takes Gildren only. He gives no receipts. He has never moved from a spot once planted. Nobody has robbed him successfully. The ones who tried found out about the brass fists.
units.tactics: Monte Haul is placed at scenario start and stays there all game. Both factions can trade with him — whoever gets there first has the advantage. He's very hard to kill (Defense 5, ignores first 2 damage per activation), hits back hard, and goes into Lockdown at low health rather than dying. Don't waste activations trying to destroy him.

units.name: Canyon Skeleton
units.lore: No one buried them right, and now the canyon is their business. Canyon Skeletons rise from collapsed mine shafts, dry riverbeds, and unmarked graves wherever the ground has been disturbed enough to let something out. They carry whatever they died with—rusty blades, old pickaxes, shards of glass tied to sticks—and they move with a horrible, clicking purpose. They don't tire. They don't flinch. They just walk toward you until one of you stops.
units.tactics: Pressure through persistence. Canyon Skeletons are not fast and not tough on their own — but they keep coming. Relentless means every kill earns a free 2" surge, and Fearless means no Morale test ever slows them down. Field them in pairs or small clusters so Rattling Horde fires consistently. Send them at objectives and make enemies spend resources removing them.

special_mechanics.rattling_horde: While within 3" of two or more other friendly Canyon Skeletons, this unit gains +1 die on melee attacks. The bones click and clatter together in a terrible rhythm that only gets louder.

optional_upgrades.name: Ancient Blade
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Pierce. The blade pre-dates most of the armor it encounters.

optional_upgrades.name: Buckler Shield
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per round, when this unit is hit by a melee attack, it may reduce the damage by 1 (to a minimum of 0). A skeleton with a shield is a worse problem than a skeleton without one.

optional_upgrades.name: Dread Rattling
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Fear. The sound it makes — all that grinding bone and scraping metal — is something the brain rejects before the eyes finish processing it.

optional_upgrades.name: Canyon Dirt Armor
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Tough. Centuries of compressed canyon sediment have filled every joint and sealed every crack. It is denser than it looks.

supplemental_abilities.name: Skeleton Archer
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Some skeletons rose mid-draw and never put the bow down. Skeleton Archers lose the melee surge of Relentless but gain a 12" ranged attack — haunting back-line pressure that never retreats and never runs dry.

supplemental_abilities.name: Skeleton Champion
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: An old officer that went into the ground with its rank still on. The Skeleton Champion is tougher, more frightening, and holds nearby skeletons in a loose and terrible formation. It is the one you deal with first — or the one that costs you dearly for not doing so.

special_mechanics.dead_generals_will: Once per round, while within 6" of two or more friendly Canyon Skeletons, this unit may direct one of them to move 3" — even if it has already activated this round. The click of its sword against its ribcage is the only order they need.

units.name: Dog Spider
units.lore: Dog spiders are canyon ambush predators the size of wolves. They skitter across rocks and wagon trails alike, striking from shadows before retreating into cracks in the stone.
units.tactics: Fast ambusher. Strike isolated targets and retreat before heavy units can respond.

optional_upgrades.name: Web Spitter
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain a web spit attack (Range 6"). On a successful hit the target becomes Pinned.

optional_upgrades.name: Burrower
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per game, this model may emerge anywhere within 8" of its starting position that is not within 3" of an enemy.

supplemental_abilities.name: Thyrfang Spider
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A Dog Spider warped by feeding on Thyr crystals. Its fangs glow faintly and its attacks grow more savage the more blood it spills.

units.name: Feratu
units.lore: Tall, pale blood drinkers hiding behind civility and shadow. In Coffin Canyon they hunt selectively, feeding on the living and growing bolder with every kill.
units.tactics: A scaling predator. Early game: isolate Rabble and wounded targets. Late game: Blood Feast dice turn it into a nightmare if unchecked.

optional_upgrades.name: Piercing Bite
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Pierce.

optional_upgrades.name: Brutal Blow
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Brutal Blow.

optional_upgrades.name: Thyr Frenzy
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Thyr Devourer and Thyr Frenzy. Quality cannot exceed 6.

supplemental_abilities.name: Feratu Stalker
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A feral night-hunter that abandons all civility. Even Skull Rangers cannot befriend it.

supplemental_abilities.name: Feratu Bride
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A charming predator that draws victims close before feeding.

supplemental_abilities.name: Feratu Gentleman
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: An aristocratic hunter who studies prey before striking. Quality cannot exceed 6.

units.name: Golem
units.lore: Constructs stitched, sculpted, or forged into life by the strange sciences of Coffin Canyon. Some are academic experiments, others relics of industry, and some simply refuse to stop moving.
units.tactics: Durable front-line anchor. Choose a material variant to define how the Golem behaves on the battlefield.

supplemental_abilities.name: Flesh Golem
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A grotesque monsterologist construct stitched from grave-rotted remains. Flesh golems stagger forward with stubborn determination.

supplemental_abilities.name: Clay Golem
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Shaped from canyon clay and kiln-fired mud, these silent guardians once watched over forgotten shrines and camps.

supplemental_abilities.name: Iron Golem
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A brutal iron construct of forgotten industry. When destroyed, it leaves behind scattered Spare Parts.

units.name: Halloween Golem
units.lore: Obsolete mechanical pumpkin-headed men built to bring order to the wilderness — cutting trees and tending farms. Now they wander about looking for trees to accost, and Thyr.
units.tactics: Reliable blockers that grind forward and absorb punishment while leaving salvage behind.

optional_upgrades.name: Keen Axe
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Pierce.

supplemental_abilities.name: Rusty Halloween Golem
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A rusting piece of trash held together by habit and hope.

units.name: Crag Thrallo
units.lore: Once forced to haul stone, iron, and scrap for cruel masters, Thrallos eventually learned that chains break easier than bones. Now they roam junkyards and gravel pits, smashing machines apart and drinking whatever strange brew they find in rusted barrels.
units.tactics: Forward bruiser. Thrallo thrives in messy brawls and tight terrain. Charge into the center, absorb punishment, and smash anything fragile.

optional_upgrades.name: Rebar Club
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Brutal Blow. A length of twisted construction steel makes every hit devastating.

optional_upgrades.name: Scrap Armor
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Bolted plates, wagon doors, and furnace lids form crude protection.

supplemental_abilities.name: Doomshine Thrallo
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Fueled by stolen Doomshine. Gains +1 Quality but must move toward the nearest enemy each activation if able.

units.name: Gulch Minotaur
units.lore: A bull-headed ruin-dweller that knows every corridor in the old stoneworks. If it charges, it's because you're already lost.
units.tactics: Charge specialist. Use terrain density to set up devastating Impact lines through corridors and ruins.

optional_upgrades.name: Huge Battle Axe
optional_upgrades.desc_short: The Minotaur carries a massive rusted executioner's axe. Gains Pierce.

supplemental_abilities.name: Labyrinth Bull
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Years of stalking ruins have honed its instincts. After destroying an enemy in melee, the Minotaur may immediately move 2".

units.name: Patchwork Wonder
units.lore: A creature of many parts, sewn together with string and wire and sparked to life by aethyr. Sometimes cloth. Sometimes muscle and gristle. Always strange.
units.tactics: Close in fast, hit hard, and keep pressure. Patchwork thrives in messy fights and punishes anyone who tries to bully monsters off objectives.

optional_upgrades.name: Aethyr Surge
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Hex.

optional_upgrades.name: Heavy Limbs
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains an additional Brutal Blow die.

supplemental_abilities.name: Sackcloth Patchwork
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A cheap and unstable cloth-stuffed construct.

units.name: Owlbear
units.lore: A hulking canyon predator with the body of a bear and the eyes of a hunting owl. When it screeches across the gulch walls, even seasoned Rangers remember they are prey.
units.tactics: Shock predator. Charge into exposed enemies and break lines with Impact and brutal melee.

optional_upgrades.name: Iron-Breaking Talons
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Pierce.

optional_upgrades.name: Blood Scent
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Furious.

supplemental_abilities.name: Mama Owlbear
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A territorial mother defending her young. Gains +1 Defense and Furious.

supplemental_abilities.name: Baby Owlbear
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A young and unpredictable owlbear cub. Fast, loud, and prone to panic.

units.name: Chimera
units.lore: A stitched-together legend that shouldn't survive in the dry valley—lion muscle, goat stubbornness, and serpent spite. Yet it prowls Coffin Canyon anyway, breathing dust and eating regrets.
units.tactics: Centerpiece brute. Push it into the center of the fight where Furious can trigger repeatedly. Chimera thrives in chaotic multi-unit engagements.

optional_upgrades.name: Serpent Tail
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Poison. A venomous serpent head lashes from the creature's tail.

optional_upgrades.name: Infernal Breath
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Hell Spray. The lion head exhales a cone of burning aethyr.

supplemental_abilities.name: Alpha Chimera
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: An ancient canyon apex predator whose hide is scarred from countless battles.

units.name: Dust King
units.lore: Ancient bandaged dead-men, risen from forbidden chemical ruins. Dust Kings wander out of collapsed tombs and sunken chambers, chewing fresh flesh when they find it.
units.tactics: Slow, durable melee pressure. Park them in ruins or choke points and force enemies to deal with Fear and grinding attacks.

optional_upgrades.name: Brutal Fear
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit upgrades Fear to Brutal Fear.

optional_upgrades.name: Toughest
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit upgrades Tougher to Toughest.

optional_upgrades.name: Thyr Frenzy
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Thyr Devourer and Thyr Frenzy. Quality cannot exceed 6.

supplemental_abilities.name: Ruinbound
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: If this unit begins its activation within 6" of a Ruin or Ancient Structure terrain, it gains +1 Defense until end of activation.

supplemental_abilities.name: Dust King Revenant
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A more powerful risen form. Tomb-Curse forces Morale tests on engaged enemies.

special_mechanics.ruinbound: If this unit begins its activation within 6" of a Ruin or Ancient Structure terrain, it gains +1 Defense until end of activation.
special_mechanics.tomb_curse: When an enemy ends its activation engaged with this unit, that enemy must test Morale.

supplemental_abilities.name: Dust Emperor
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: The apex Bloodless commander. Royal Tomb-Ward and Dust Command make it a force multiplier for undead warbands.

special_mechanics.ruinbound: If this unit begins its activation within 6" of a Ruin or Ancient Structure terrain, it gains +1 Defense until end of activation.
special_mechanics.royal_tomb_ward: Once per game: choose one Ruin or Ancient Structure terrain within 12". Until end of round, enemies treat that terrain as Hazardous.
special_mechanics.dust_command: Once per round, when a friendly Bloodless unit within 8" fails a Morale test, it may reroll.

units.name: Kelpie
units.lore: A damp horse-beast out of place in the dry canyon. Desperate, thirsty, and dangerous—pulling prey into shade, mud, or deeper trouble.
units.tactics: Hit-and-run controller. Grapple key targets off objectives or into danger zones. Works best near water terrain.

optional_upgrades.name: Deepwater Pull
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Impact.

optional_upgrades.name: Bog Hunter
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Blood in the Air.

supplemental_abilities.name: River Kelpie
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A strong river hunter with greater endurance.

supplemental_abilities.name: Starved Kelpie
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A desperate desert wanderer running on instinct and spite.

units.name: Lionvulture
units.lore: A four-legged wild beast—more animal than intellect—nesting near Thyr outcroppings where it can guard and feed.
units.tactics: Flank, pounce, and retreat. Use speed to contest Thyr and punish isolated models. Avoid prolonged brawls.

optional_upgrades.name: Savage Pounce
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Impact.

optional_upgrades.name: Blood Scent
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Blood in the Air.

supplemental_abilities.name: Alpha Lionvulture
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Dominant territorial predator leading the pack.

units.name: Valley Centaur
units.lore: Nomads of the high mesas, valley centaurs roam the broken trails and wind-cut ridges of Coffin Canyon. Some will bargain for water, salt, or news. Most prefer to run strangers off with a warning arrow.
units.tactics: Mobile skirmisher. Suppress dangerous enemies from range, then reposition quickly to stay out of melee.

optional_upgrades.name: Longbow
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Replace this unit's weapon with a Longbow (Range 18"). Valley centaurs are legendary canyon archers.

supplemental_abilities.name: Bloodhoof Centaur
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: No one knows where the Bloodhoof Centaurs came from. These enormous ogre-centaurs charge like living avalanches, crushing bone and stone beneath their hooves.

units.name: Lamia
units.lore: A mirage given teeth. Lamias haunt the dry places where trails fade and water rumors begin. Travelers who follow their laughter often vanish into the heat shimmer.
units.tactics: Control and attrition. Hex dangerous enemies, fracture morale with psychic attacks, and finish off weakened prey.

optional_upgrades.name: Mirage Veil
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per round when this unit is targeted by a ranged attack, it may move 3".

optional_upgrades.name: Hypnotic Eyes
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Fear.

supplemental_abilities.name: Dune Lamia
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: An ancient Lamia that has ruled the dunes for decades. Its whispers carry further, and its magic cuts deeper.

units.name: Medusa
units.lore: A ruin-queen who wanders the shattered temples and dry river shrines of Coffin Canyon. Her gaze does not kill outright—it turns courage into hesitation and flesh slowly into stone. The canyon is littered with statues that once had names.
units.tactics: Debuff controller. Keep distance and maintain line of sight while weakening enemies with petrifying gaze.

units.name: Bête Herdbeast
units.lore: Huge, shaggy horned beasts that smell faintly of candy. Bêtes are dream-made giants—often gentle—yet terrifying when startled.
units.tactics: Anchor the center. Use its footprint to block lanes and protect Thyr. Avoid unnecessary fights; win by surviving, holding ground, or being Escorted.

optional_upgrades.name: Thick Hide
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Toughest.

optional_upgrades.name: Terrifying Bulk
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Brutal Fear.

optional_upgrades.name: Thyr Sated
optional_upgrades.desc_short: This unit gains Thyr Sated.

supplemental_abilities.name: Gentle Giant
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Will not declare attacks against Monster Rangers unless attacked first.

supplemental_abilities.name: Stampede
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: If this unit fails a Morale test it immediately moves up to its Move in a straight line. It may pass through smaller models, applying Impact normally.

supplemental_abilities.name: Baby Bête
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A small, skittish Bête calf still learning the world is dangerous.

supplemental_abilities.name: Mama Bête
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A fierce mother whose patience ends the moment her calf is threatened.

special_mechanics.pack_protector: When a Baby Bête within 6" is targeted by an attack, Mama Bête may immediately move up to 4" toward the attacker.

units.name: Klowna
units.weapon: Titan Talons, Twin Maw & Triple-Leg Lunge
units.lore: A towering chitinous horror—nearly 14 feet tall—with two heads, three legs, and enormous talons. It crawls out of an old wreck like a bad joke that refuses to end.
units.tactics: A shared catastrophe. Spawn it mid-game to spike tension. Force everyone to decide: keep scoring, or deal with the Titan before it eats the canyon.

notes: Befriending Restriction (Optional Rule): Undead and Incorporeal monsters marked 'SkullRangerBefriendOnly' may only be Befriended by the Monster Rangers' Skull Ranger branch.
Titan Rule: Titans are scenario events. When a Titan enters play, all factions must reassess priorities.
Temperament is used by the scenario engine to determine monster AI behavior when uncontrolled.
Bloodless monsters are immune to Poison and Blood in the Air effects.
